articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Hires Jason Clark as CFO Amid Growth Push
articles.summary: Family Entertainment Group, one of the largest FEC operators in the US, has appointed Jason Clark as Chief Financial Officer effective May 26, 2026. Clark brings extensive financial leadership from the restaurant, hospitality, and entertainment sectors. The hire signals FEG's continued organizational investment and likely expansion ambitions.

articles.title: FACE Amusement Group Opens 50,000-Sq-Ft Guy Fieri-Branded FEC in Myrtle Beach
articles.summary: FACE Amusement Group has officially opened Guy Fieri's Downtown Flavortown at Myrtle Beach Mall, a 50,000-square-foot dining and entertainment destination built in partnership with the celebrity chef. The venue expands on the brand's flagship location in Pigeon Forge, demonstrating the continued viability of celebrity-IP-driven FEC concepts. This opening highlights how entertainment-dining hybrids are capturing prime retail mall real estate.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Periscope Invests in Amusement Connect
articles.summary: Periscope, a private equity firm, has made an investment in Amusement Connect, signaling growing institutional interest in FEC technology and management platforms. Investment in amusement-focused software infrastructure suggests the sector is attracting serious capital. FEC operators should watch how this funding shapes product development and market competition.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Strong Global Growth
articles.summary: A new market report highlights that the location-based entertainment sector is experiencing robust worldwide expansion. The findings are broadly positive for FEC operators and investors tracking industry momentum. Growing consumer demand for out-of-home experiences continues to support new venue development and attraction investment.

articles.title: Madison's 'The Gallery' Entertainment District to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new large-scale entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison this summer, adding to the wave of mixed-use FEC-anchored developments across the US. Projects of this scale reflect sustained developer confidence in entertainment as a retail traffic driver. FEC operators and suppliers should monitor this project as a potential new market opportunity.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Expands into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Venue
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is continuing its aggressive nationwide rollout with a new location in Maryland, reinforcing the brand's position as one of the fastest-growing FEC chains in the country. The expansion underscores the strength of the trampoline park and adventure park segment in capturing suburban family demographics. Competing operators should note Urban Air's real estate strategy and market penetration pace.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Previews New Salem, Oregon Location
articles.summary: Urban Air has opened a new Adventure Park location in Salem, Oregon, with local media offering a sneak peek of the facility's attractions and layout. The opening adds to the brand's expanding Pacific Northwest footprint. For regional FEC operators, Urban Air's continued growth represents both competitive pressure and a market validation signal.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Major New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A large new entertainment venue featuring go-karts and laser tag is being developed in Plymouth, England, adding to the growing number of multi-attraction FEC concepts opening across the UK. The project reflects the continued international expansion of the FEC format beyond North America. Operators and suppliers tracking UK market growth will find this development noteworthy.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool
articles.summary: UK bowling and entertainment chain Tenpin has opened a new venue in Blackpool, expanding its multi-attraction FEC footprint in the country. Tenpin's ongoing growth demonstrates the resilience of the bowling-anchored entertainment center model in the UK market. The opening is another data point supporting the broader trend of entertainment center expansion in British leisure destinations.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre, London
articles.summary: A major new entertainment venue has opened at Brent Cross shopping centre in London, representing a significant investment in the UK's growing FEC market. The opening continues the trend of shopping centres anchoring retail spaces with large-format entertainment destinations. FEC developers and investors should note the scale and mall-integration model being deployed.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Drive Miraculous Location-Based Entertainment Licensing Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to spearhead location-based entertainment licensing for the Miraculous IP across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific. The deal opens doors for FEC operators in those regions to develop Miraculous-themed attractions, escape rooms, and interactive experiences. IP-driven FEC concepts continue to be a strong traffic and revenue driver, making this licensing deal worth tracking for operators considering new themed offerings.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Progresses at Jacksonville's Southside Commons Development
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing construction within the Southside Commons mixed-use development in Jacksonville, Florida. The project adds to the growing list of adventure park concepts being embedded in larger retail and entertainment districts. FEC operators and real estate investors should note the continued pairing of adventure park concepts with mixed-use developments.

articles.title: Red Raion Launches Multi-Format CGI Film 'Dragon's Gold – The Chase' for FEC Attractions
articles.summary: Red Raion has released Dragon's Gold – The Chase, a new CGI film available in 5D, VR, dome, and flying theatre formats, designed for high-throughput family venues. The content is engineered for repeatability and broad audience appeal, making it a practical option for FEC operators looking to refresh existing media-based attractions. The multi-format flexibility lowers the barrier to entry for smaller venues with varying technology setups.

articles.title: Brogent Debuts 360-Degree 'R-Ride' Motion Theatre and Announces Five New Southeast Asian Projects at IAAPA Asia
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies unveiled its R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026, a motion theatre featuring a 360-degree panoramic screen and full rotation system offering an immersive, high-intensity experience. The company also announced a partnership with a major Japanese IP and five new attraction projects across Southeast Asia. FEC operators and attraction investors should watch R-Ride as a potential next-generation draw for venues seeking differentiated, technology-forward experiences.

articles.title: Brogent's R-Ride and Japanese IP Alliance Signal New Era for Media-Based Attractions
articles.summary: Amusement Today's coverage of Brogent's IAAPA Asia showcase provides additional detail on the R-Ride motion theatre and the company's strategic Japanese IP collaboration. Five Southeast Asian FEC and theme park projects were also announced, reflecting strong regional investment momentum. Operators looking at next-generation motion theatre upgrades will want to track Brogent's commercial rollout timeline.

articles.title: Riot at Raleigh Urban Air Forces Early Closure, Three Charged
articles.summary: A disturbance at an Urban Air Family Fun Center in Raleigh, North Carolina escalated into a riot, resulting in the venue shutting down early and three women being charged by police. The incident highlights the ongoing challenge FEC operators face managing guest behavior, particularly in high-traffic, youth-oriented venues. Operators should review crowd management policies, security staffing, and incident response protocols in light of events like this.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Offers Free Adventure Zone Admission for International Day of Play, Launches June Fundraiser
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese is celebrating International Day of Play with complimentary Adventure Zone admission and a month-long fundraiser in partnership with The Toy Foundation. The promotion is a strong example of FEC brands leveraging cause-marketing and community engagement to drive foot traffic. Operators can take note of this strategy for building brand goodwill and attracting families during the competitive summer season.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville Launches Monthly 'Playmaker's Pour' Cocktail Program
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville has introduced a monthly themed cocktail program featuring patriotic beverages for June and July, tying seasonal drinks to its social gaming concept. The initiative is a practical example of how adult-oriented FECs are using beverage programming to drive repeat visits and increase per-capita spend. Operators of bar-arcade and social entertainment venues can draw inspiration from this low-cost, high-impact promotional tactic.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Bowling Alley Closes Permanently
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has closed its doors, redirecting customers to a nearby Appleton bowling center. The closure is a reminder of the ongoing consolidation pressures facing standalone bowling centers that lack diversified entertainment offerings. FEC operators in the bowling segment should monitor closure trends as an indicator of market conditions and potential acquisition opportunities.

articles.title: Christie Launches XP Series LED with 10-Year Warranty for Immersive Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Christie has unveiled its XP Series LED display platform, built for high-reliability environments including immersive entertainment spaces, backed by an industry-leading 10-year warranty. The system features advanced color reproduction and intelligent processing suited to VR, simulation, and themed attraction applications relevant to FEC tech upgrades. The long warranty and commercial-grade reliability make this a compelling option for operators investing in permanent LED-based installations.

articles.title: Oregon Robot Vendors Automate Business Operations While Claiming Jobs Are Safe
articles.summary: New robotics dealers in Oregon are marketing automation solutions to businesses including entertainment and hospitality venues, asserting that robots supplement rather than replace human workers. For FEC operators facing persistent labor cost pressures, robotic automation for tasks like food service or ticketing is an increasingly viable consideration. The article reflects a broader industry conversation about balancing labor efficiency with guest experience.

articles.title: London-Style Social Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, foosball, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, inspired by London's social entertainment boom. The format mirrors trends already well established in the US FEC market, suggesting accelerating global adoption of the social gaming venue model. Operators and investors tracking international expansion of the FEC concept will find this European market development significant.

articles.title: Arizona's Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026 Ranked
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its ranking of Arizona's top indoor entertainment attractions for 2026, spotlighting the competitive landscape for FEC operators in the state. Such rankings influence consumer decision-making and can directly impact foot traffic for featured and excluded venues alike. FEC operators in the region should monitor these lists as both a competitive intelligence tool and a marketing opportunity.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates to Larger Fulton Market
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, an arcade and dessert concept, has moved from Holts Summit to a new location in Fulton, Missouri, likely seeking a larger customer base. The relocation reflects the strategic growth decisions small FEC operators face when balancing market size with operating costs. Independent FEC owners can draw lessons from this example of using relocation as a growth lever.

articles.title: ProSlide Premieres PowerRAPIDS, a New Category of Water Ride, in Vietnam
articles.summary: ProSlide Technology has globally debuted PowerRAPIDS at Aquatopia Water Park in Vietnam, introducing a new class of water ride that combines traditional slides with dynamic floating river channels for a sustained thrill experience. The innovation moves beyond conventional water slide design and could influence future waterpark attraction investment decisions. While primarily relevant to waterpark operators, FEC facilities with aquatic components may find PowerRAPIDS a compelling draw for differentiation.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Building Modular Travelling Exhibitions for Global Venues
articles.summary: A detailed industry guide from five global touring exhibition experts covers best practices for modular design, logistics, staffing documentation, and technology future-proofing for travelling entertainment experiences. FEC operators considering pop-up or touring attractions will find actionable advice on reducing freight costs, ensuring local technical serviceability, and enabling remote content updates. The guide is particularly relevant as more FECs explore rotating or licensed experiential activations.

articles.title: Minecraft Pop-Up Experience Arrives at Alton Towers, Showing IP Activation Potential for FECs
articles.summary: Merlin Entertainments has launched a Minecraft-branded pop-up experience at Alton Towers featuring interactive mob characters, themed activities, an in-game reward trail, and exclusive merchandise. The activation demonstrates how gaming IP can be translated into compelling physical entertainment experiences that drive incremental visits and merchandise revenue. FEC operators and event programmers can draw inspiration from this model for temporary branded experiences.

articles.title: Bear River Entertainment Zone Draws Chamber of Commerce Interest in Northern California
articles.summary: Chamber members from Fortuna and Eureka visited the newly opened Bear River Casino Resort entertainment zone, highlighting growing regional interest in FEC-style destinations outside major metro areas. The visit suggests local business communities are recognizing entertainment venues as economic development anchors. Operators and developers targeting secondary markets will find this an encouraging signal.
